---
title: "Data"
pagetitle: "Skein Lite: Data"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import json
S = json.load(open("../evaluation/reports/site_stats.json"))
```

::: {.eyebrow}
One domain pack, three evidence stores
:::

Everything Sara can say comes from three stores: a hybrid vector index, a knowledge graph, and a governed metric layer. All three build from one folder, `domains/apparel_ecommerce/`, whose `domain.yaml` manifest declares the schema, PII columns, graph edges, and metrics. The demo pack carries `{python} S["catalog"]["products"]` products in `{python} S["catalog"]["variants"]` variants.

## The flow

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  subgraph Pack [domains/apparel_ecommerce/]
    direction TB
    ST["seed/structured/*.csv<br/>products · sales · suppliers · stores"]
    UN["seed/unstructured/*.jsonl<br/>descriptions · reviews · guides · orders"]
    MAN["domain.yaml<br/>schema · PII · edges · metrics"]
  end
  ST --> BR
  UN --> CH
  subgraph Medallion [DuckDB + dbt]
    direction TB
    BR[bronze<br/>raw, lineage] --> SI[silver<br/>typed + PII masked] --> GO[gold<br/>curated marts]
  end
  subgraph Stores [What Sara reads]
    direction TB
    MET[governed metrics<br/>read-only DuckDB]
    GR[knowledge graph<br/>Neo4j]
    VEC[hybrid vectors<br/>Qdrant]
  end
  GO --> MET
  GO --> GR
  CH[chunk + context prefix<br/>Cohere embed-v4.0] --> VEC

  classDef pack fill:#e9f3f4,stroke:#0f7b84,color:#13191e;
  classDef gold fill:#f8f2e6,stroke:#c8791f,color:#13191e;
  class ST,UN,MAN pack;
  class GO gold;
```

## The medallion

The structured CSVs run through a dbt medallion in local DuckDB, so it needs no infrastructure and reproduces byte for byte.

| Layer | Holds | Guarded by |
|---|---|---|
| Bronze | Raw rows, verbatim | Lineage back to the source file |
| Silver | Typed columns, PII masked to a `masked:` hash | A generic `is_masked` dbt test that fails the build on a raw value |
| Gold | The curated marts Sara and the dashboards query | `not_null`, `unique`, `relationships` tests |

Two extra guards keep the lakehouse honest. A parity test in CI asserts the dbt-built gold matches an in-app Python builder exactly, so the two ways of producing gold cannot quietly diverge. And dbt exposures name every downstream consumer, so lineage answers "what does this table feed".

The demo's structured tables carry no personal data. Customer PII lives in the order documents on the unstructured side, where a deterministic name-plus-email gate decides disclosure (see [Reliability](reliability.qmd)). A pack that declares a PII column gets the masking test with no engine change.

## The unstructured path

Descriptions, reviews, guides, and order documents are chunked with a short context prefix naming the product they belong to, embedded with Cohere `embed-v4.0`, and indexed in Qdrant as dense plus sparse. That is why "a warm rain jacket" and an exact style name both land.

## The knowledge graph

Nodes and typed edges build from the gold tables, never by hand, so the graph and the tables cannot disagree. "Which supplier makes the Cloud Hoodie" is one hop over the `SUPPLIES` edge, returned as authoritative grounding. Traversals are allowlisted, no free-form Cypher, which keeps the graph injection-safe.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Honest scope.** `SUPPLIES` is fully populated. The product-to-store `SOLD_AT` edge derives from a handful of historical sales and returns nothing for most of the catalog. It is a modeled capability waiting on real inventory data, and it is labeled as such.
:::

## Governed metrics

When a shopper asks "what's the cheapest legging" or whether a size is in stock, the number comes from `metrics.yaml`: one definition per metric, shared by the assistant, the eval harness, and the dashboards. The model fills declared slots, a resolver runs one parameterized SELECT on a read-only DuckDB connection, and the result comes back as its own cited evidence. No free-form SQL, no invented numbers.

## The data lifecycle is human-triggered

The corpus never mutates itself. Two jobs propose, a person disposes:

| Job | Proposes | Who acts |
|---|---|---|
| Consolidation (`mlops/consolidate.py`) | A knowledge pack distilled from operator-closed answers, plus gaps worth filling | A human edits and approves; only then does the flywheel index it |
| Staleness (`mlops/staleness.py`) | A refresh list of verified chunks older than the threshold | A human approves the re-index; nothing expires on its own |

Candidate knowledge comes only from answers a person already verified, never from an LLM rewriting stored content, because a model that continuously rewrites its own memory corrupts it.
